Settings reference¶

The following is the project settings module (shown for documentation purposes):

  1"""
  2Django settings for the DispatchDjango project.
  3Provides configuration for database, security, and application modules.
  4"""
  5
  6from pathlib import Path
  7
  8# Build paths inside the project like this: BASE_DIR / 'subdir'.
  9BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
 10
 11# SECURITY WARNING: keep the secret key used in production secret!
 12SECRET_KEY = 'django-insecure-your-secret-key-here'
 13
 14# SECURITY WARNING: don't run with debug turned on in production!
 15DEBUG = True
 16ALLOWED_HOSTS = []
 17
 18# Application definition
 19INSTALLED_APPS = [
 20    'django.contrib.admin',
 21    'django.contrib.auth',
 22    'django.contrib.contenttypes',
 23    'django.contrib.sessions',
 24    'django.contrib.messages',
 25    'django.contrib.staticfiles',
 26    'rest_framework',
 27    'dispatch_app',
 28]
 29
 30# Custom User Model definition
 31AUTH_USER_MODEL = 'dispatch_app.CustomUser'
 32
 33MIDDLEWARE = [
 34    'django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware',
 35    'django.contrib.sessions.middleware.SessionMiddleware',
 36    'django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware',
 37    'django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware',
 38    'django.contrib.auth.middleware.AuthenticationMiddleware',
 39    'django.contrib.messages.middleware.MessageMiddleware',
 40    'django.middleware.clickjacking.XFrameOptionsMiddleware',
 41]
 42
 43ROOT_URLCONF = 'dispatch_core.urls'
 44
 45TEMPLATES = [
 46    {
 47        'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
 48        'DIRS': [BASE_DIR / 'templates'],
 49        'APP_DIRS': True,
 50        'OPTIONS': {
 51            'context_processors': [
 52                'django.template.context_processors.debug',
 53                'django.template.context_processors.request',
 54                'django.contrib.auth.context_processors.auth',
 55                'django.contrib.messages.context_processors.messages',
 56            ],
 57        },
 58    },
 59]
 60
 61WSGI_APPLICATION = 'dispatch_core.wsgi.application'
 62
 63# Database configuration
 64# The project has been configured to use MariaDB
 65# instead of the default SQLite.
 66DATABASES = {
 67    'default': {
 68        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.mysql',
 69        'NAME': 'dispatch_db',
 70        'USER': 'dispatch_admin',
 71        'PASSWORD': '***********',
 72        'HOST': '127.0.0.1',
 73        'PORT': '3306',
 74        'OPTIONS': {
 75            'init_command': "SET sql_mode='STRICT_TRANS_TABLES'",
 76        },
 77    }
 78}
 79
 80# Password validation
 81AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS = [
 82    {
 83        'NAME': 'django.contrib.auth.password_validation.'
 84                'UserAttributeSimilarityValidator'
 85    },
 86    {
 87        'NAME': 'django.contrib.auth.password_validation.'
 88                'MinimumLengthValidator'
 89    },
 90    {
 91        'NAME': 'django.contrib.auth.password_validation.'
 92                'CommonPasswordValidator'
 93    },
 94    {
 95        'NAME': 'django.contrib.auth.password_validation.'
 96                'NumericPasswordValidator'
 97    },
 98]
 99
100# Internationalization
101LANGUAGE_CODE = 'en-us'
102TIME_ZONE = 'UTC'
103USE_I18N = True
104USE_TZ = True
105
106# Static files (CSS, JavaScript, Images)
107STATIC_URL = 'static/'
108
109# Static files configuration for global static folder.
110# This helps PyCharm resolve the 'css/style.css' reference.
111STATICFILES_DIRS = [
112    BASE_DIR / "static",
113]
114
115DEFAULT_AUTO_FIELD = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'
116
117# Authentication Redirects
118# Users will be sent to the home page after login or logout.
119LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL = 'article_list'
120LOGOUT_REDIRECT_URL = 'home'
121# This ensures Django knows where the custom login page is located.
122LOGIN_URL = 'login'
123
124# Email Configuration for Development
125# Emails will be printed to the standard output (console).
126EMAIL_BACKEND = 'django.core.mail.backends.console.EmailBackend'
127DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL = 'noreply@dispatch-django.com'
128
129# X (formerly Twitter) Application Programming Interface configuration.
130# These credentials are required for the automated posting feature.
131# Replace the placeholder strings with your actual keys from the X
132# Developer Portal.
133# noinspection SpellCheckingInspection
134X_API_KEY = 'your-api-key-here'
135# noinspection SpellCheckingInspection
136X_API_SECRET_KEY = 'your-api-secret-here'
137
138# New tokens for automated posting
139# noinspection SpellCheckingInspection
140X_ACCESS_TOKEN = 'your-access-token-here'
141# noinspection SpellCheckingInspection
142X_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET = 'your-access-token-secret-here'
143# noinspection SpellCheckingInspection
144X_BEARER_TOKEN = 'your-bearer-token-here'

CSS theme file¶

  1/*
  2DispatchDjango Theme (VendorVerse Heritage):
  3Background: #402000 (Very Dark Brown)
  4Main Theme: #FF8000 (Vivid Orange)
  5Text:       #FFB000 (Light Orange/Gold)
  6Border:     #BF6000 (Darker Orange)
  7Bright:     #FFFF00 (Yellow)
  8*/
  9
 10/* Global & Input Scrollbar Styling */
 11* {
 12    scrollbar-color: #BF6000 #402000;
 13    scrollbar-width: thin;
 14}
 15
 16/* For Chrome, Edge, and Safari */
 17::-webkit-scrollbar {
 18    width: 10px;
 19    height: 10px;
 20}
 21
 22::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
 23    background: #402000;
 24}
 25
 26::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
 27    background: #BF6000;
 28    border-radius: 5px;
 29}
 30
 31::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover {
 32    background: #FF8000;
 33}
 34
 35body {
 36    background-color: #402000;
 37    color: #FFB000;
 38    font-family: 'Segoe UI', Tahoma, Geneva, Verdana, sans-serif;
 39    margin: 0;
 40    padding: 0;
 41}
 42
 43header, nav {
 44    background-color: #4D2600;
 45    border-bottom: 2px solid #BF6000;
 46    padding: 20px;
 47}
 48
 49h1, h2, h3 {
 50    color: #FF8000;
 51}
 52
 53main, .container {
 54    padding: 20px;
 55    max-width: 1200px;
 56    margin: 0 auto;
 57}
 58
 59.card, .article-card {
 60    background-color: #4D2600;
 61    border: 1px solid #BF6000;
 62    padding: 20px;
 63    margin-bottom: 20px;
 64    border-radius: 4px;
 65}
 66
 67button, .btn {
 68    background-color: #FF8000;
 69    color: #402000;
 70    border: 2px solid #BF6000;
 71    padding: 10px 20px;
 72    cursor: pointer;
 73    font-weight: bold;
 74    text-transform: uppercase;
 75    display: inline-block;
 76    text-decoration: none;
 77}
 78
 79button:hover, .btn:hover {
 80    background-color: #FFFF00 !important;
 81    color: #402000 !important;
 82    text-decoration: none;
 83}
 84
 85input, textarea, select {
 86    background-color: #593000;
 87    border: 1px solid #BF6000;
 88    color: #FFFF00;
 89    padding: 10px;
 90    margin-bottom: 10px;
 91    width: 100%;
 92    box-sizing: border-box;
 93    font-family: inherit;
 94    color-scheme: dark; /* Helps browsers choose dark system colors */
 95}
 96
 97/*
 98   Placeholder / Hint text styling.
 99   This changes the default grey text to match your theme's
100   muted orange border color.
101*/
102input::placeholder,
103textarea::placeholder {
104    color: #BF6000;
105    opacity: 0.8; /* Ensures it looks like a hint and not final text */
106}
107
108/* Text selection styling to match VendorVerse theme */
109::selection {
110    background-color: #FF8000 !important; /* Vivid Orange background */
111    color: #402000 !important;            /* Very Dark Brown text */
112}
113
114::-moz-selection {
115    background-color: #FF8000 !important; /* For Firefox */
116    color: #402000 !important;
117}
118
119select {
120    /* This tells the browser to use a dark theme for system elements */
121    color-scheme: dark;
122    /* This influences the checkbox/radio/select highlight colors */
123    accent-color: #FF8000 !important;
124    cursor: pointer;
125}
126
127/* Ensure the select dropdown elements also match the theme */
128select option {
129    background-color: #402000 !important;
130    color: #FFB000 !important;
131    padding: 10px;
132}
133
134/*
135   Hover/Checked Override:
136   We use a massive inset shadow to cover the system blue.
137   We also use text-shadow to ensure text remains dark brown
138   even if the system tries to force it to white.
139*/
140select option:hover,
141select option:focus,
142select option:checked {
143    background-color: #FF8000 !important;
144    color: #402000 !important;
145    box-shadow: 0 0 10px 1000px #FF8000 inset !important;
146    text-shadow: 0 0 0 #402000 !important;
147}
148
149/* Focus state for the dropdown */
150input:focus,
151textarea:focus,
152select:focus {
153    outline: none;
154    border-color: #FFFF00 !important;
155    background-color: #663300 !important;
156}
157
158label {
159    display: block;
160    color: #FF8000;
161    font-weight: bold;
162    margin-bottom: 5px;
163    margin-top: 15px;
164}
165
166hr {
167    border: 0;
168    height: 1px;
169    background-color: #BF6000;
170    margin: 20px 0;
171}
172
173a {
174    color: #FF8000;
175    text-decoration: none;
176}
177
178a:hover {
179    color: #FFFF00;
180}

Dispatch Django
